mnemonics.title: NCSF Project Flow
mnemonics.content: New Project → Configure → Select Template → Finish. Remember 'Never Cook Soggy Fries' for the sequence when starting a project

mnemonics.title: Core Files Memory
mnemonics.content: Remember 'MAGMA' - MainActivity, XML (layout), Gradle, Manifest, Assets. These files form the foundation like volcanic magma

mnemonics.title: AVD Setup Steps
mnemonics.content: Device → System Image → Download → Launch. 'Don't Start Learning Difficulties' for the emulator creation sequence

mnemonics.title: onCreate Lifecycle
mnemonics.content: 'Only Cats Really Enjoy Sleeping' - onCreate() is where your app first wakes up (initialization point)

acronyms.usage: Remember it's your app's first test audience - like a digital crash test dummy
Your UI blueprint - Xylophones Make Layouts (visualize musical notes arranging components)
Final packaged app - Always Pack Keenly (the bundle you ship)
Your coding workshop - Imagine Developing Everything (Android Studio)

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a tree: roots (manifests), trunk (java/kotlin), branches (res), leaves (individual files)
Picture transparent graph paper over a phone screen where you place components
Visualize a factory assembly line: code → compiler → .apk → emulator
Think of a blackbox flight recorder - it's your app's continuous diary

analogies.analogy: Like building a house: Blueprint (XML), Contractor (Gradle), Foreman (MainActivity), Inspection (AVD)
A car test track - lets you test drive apps without real device risks
Like a master chef - mixes ingredients (code, resources) and cooks (compiles) your APK dish
App's ID card - lists permissions, activities, and essential characteristics
